popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description: He was Sheriff of the county in 1437, and frequently member of parliament for Derbyshire during the reign of Henry VI, dying about 1450. He married Joan, daughter of Sir John Bagot, of Blithfield. Of …

zh.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon是John Curzon和Margery Montgomery的兒子，於1270年出生在英國德比夏爾凱德萊斯頓。他和Joan Baggott至少有6個兒子和11個女兒。他去世於1330年享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭德比夏爾凱德萊斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon是John Curzon和Margery Montgomery的儿子，于1270年出生在英国德比夏爾凱德萊斯頓。他和Joan Baggott至少有6个儿子和11个女儿。他去世于1330年享年60岁，埋葬在英国英格兰德比夏爾凱德萊斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Curzon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Notre-Dame-de-Courson in Calvados, France, which was named with the Gallo-Roman personal name Curtius (from curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -o, genitive -onis. There is also a place called Curzon in Vendée, but this is not the source of the English surname.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon was born in 1270, in Kedleston, Derbyshire, England as the son of John Curzon and Margery Montgomery. He had at least 6 sons and 11 daughters with Joan Baggott. He died in 1330, at the age of 60, and was buried in Kedleston, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon est né en 1270, à Kedleston, Derbyshire, Angleterre, fils de John Curzon et Margery Montgomery. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 11 filles avec Joan Baggott. Il est décédé en 1330, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Kedleston, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon wurde 1270 in Kedleston, Derbyshire, England, als Sohn von John Curzon und Margery Montgomery geboren. Er hatte mit Joan Baggott mindestens 6 Söhne und 11 Töchter. Er starb 1330 im Alter von 60 Jahren und wurde in Kedleston, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon è nato il nel 1270 nel Kedleston, Derbyshire, England come figlio di John Curzon e Margery Montgomery. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 11 figlie con Joan Baggott. Morì nel 1330, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Kedleston, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Kedleston, Derbyshire, England: イギリスデルブイシュアイアケドレストン
ja.All Saints Churchyard, Kedleston, Amber: イギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアケドレストン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Curzonは1270年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアケドレストンでJohn CurzonとMargery Montgomeryの息子として生まれた。彼はJoan Baggottとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と11人の娘をもうけた。彼は1330年に60歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアケドレストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon(은)는 1270년 영국 더비실르 케들레스턴에서 John Curzon(와)과 Margery Montgomery의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Joan Baggott(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1330년 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 케들레스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon nasceu em 1270, em Kedleston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra filho de John Curzon e Margery Montgomery. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 11 filhas com Joan Baggott. Ele faleceu em 1330, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Kedleston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon родился в 1270 г. в местности кедлестон, дирбишир, Англия. Его родителей звали John Curzon и Margery Montgomery. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 11 дочерей от Joan Baggott. Он умер в 1330 г. в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности кедлестон, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Curzon nació en 1270, en Kedleston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra como hijo de John Curzon y Margery Montgomery. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 11 hijas con Joan Baggott. Murió en 1330, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Kedleston, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
